entries.name: LRTDepositPool
entries.description: User deposit entrypoint and asset-routing hub. Holds idle LST and ETH balances, mints rsETH on deposit at the LRTOracle quote, maintains the NodeDelegator queue used to forward balances to the EigenLayer side, and exposes the protocol-wide per-asset accounting view (getTotalAssetDeposits / getAssetDistributionData / getETHDistributionData), which sums balances across this contract, every entry in nodeDelegatorQueue (via NodeDelegator getAssetBalance / getAssetUnstaking / getEffectivePodShares), the LRT_UNSTAKING_VAULT-registered address, and the LRT_CONVERTER-registered address. All role checks consult IAccessControl on the address held in lrtConfig. Role-by-role gates: DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE: setMaxNegligibleAmount, setMinAmountToDeposit, updateMaxNodeDelegatorLimit, addNodeDelegatorContractToQueue (rejects beyond maxNodeDelegatorLimit), removeNodeDelegatorContractFromQueue and removeManyNodeDelegatorContractsFromQueue (each rejects unless every supported-asset balance plus EigenPod shares plus the unstaking-queue balance on the NDC are at or below maxNegligibleAmount), unpause. MANAGER: stakeEthForStETH (calls Lido submit() with this contract's ETH balance), maxApproveToLRTConverter, revokeApproveToLRTConverter. OPERATOR_ROLE: swapETHForAssetWithinDepositPool, swapAssetForETHWithinDepositPool (both quote against LRTOracle.getAssetPrice). ASSET_TRANSFER_ROLE: transferAssetToNodeDelegator, transferETHToNodeDelegator, transferAssetToLRTUnstakingVault, transferETHToLRTUnstakingVault. PAUSER_ROLE: pause. Three non-obvious behaviours: (1) only depositETH and depositAsset (the user-facing entrypoints) carry whenNotPaused; the admin / manager / operator / asset-transfer functions all run while this contract is paused. (2) The deposit-cap check is asymmetric for ETH: for ERC20 assets _beforeDeposit reverts when current totalAssetDeposits + amount > depositLimitByAsset, but for native ETH it reverts only if the current totalAssetDeposits is already strictly above the limit, so a single ETH deposit can push the total over the cap as long as the cap was not already exceeded before the call. (3) getRsETHAmountToMint reads LRTOracle.getAssetPrice and rsETHPrice with no bounds check, so a corrupted oracle reading translates directly into the rsETH minted to the depositor.

lrtConfig.description: Address of the LRTConfig used for every role check on this contract (onlyLRTAdmin, onlyLRTManager, onlyLRTOperator, onlyAssetTransferRole, onlyRole(PAUSER_ROLE)) and every registry lookup (getContract for LRT_ORACLE, LRT_UNSTAKING_VAULT, LRT_CONVERTER, plus rsETH() and getLSTToken(ST_ETH_TOKEN) reads). Set in initialize and not updateable by any function on this contract.

paused.description: Inherited PausableUpgradeable state. Set true by pause() (PAUSER_ROLE) and by any external caller that holds PAUSER_ROLE on lrtConfig and invokes pause() directly. Set false by unpause() (DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE). Only blocks the user-facing depositETH and depositAsset entrypoints; admin, manager, operator and asset-transfer functions on this contract continue to execute while paused.

maxNodeDelegatorLimit.description: Cap on nodeDelegatorQueue.length. addNodeDelegatorContractToQueue reverts when adding would cross this cap. Initialized to 10 in initialize and updateable only by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE via updateMaxNodeDelegatorLimit; the setter has no upper bound.

minAmountToDeposit.description: Minimum acceptable deposit amount enforced by _beforeDeposit; depositETH and depositAsset revert with InvalidAmountToDeposit when the supplied amount is zero or below this. Updateable only by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE via setMinAmountToDeposit.

maxNegligibleAmount.description: Dust threshold (in wei, 18-decimal asset units) gating NodeDelegator removal. removeNodeDelegatorContractFromQueue and removeManyNodeDelegatorContractsFromQueue revert unless the target NodeDelegator's residual native ETH balance, getEffectivePodShares, getAssetUnstaking(ETH_TOKEN), and per-supported-asset getAssetBalance + getAssetUnstaking are each at or below this. Updateable only by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE via setMaxNegligibleAmount.

getETHDistributionData.description: Per-bucket native ETH accounting snapshot used to drive getTotalAssetDeposits(ETH_TOKEN). Sums: this contract's balance (ethLyingInDepositPool); for every NDC in nodeDelegatorQueue, the NDC's balance plus its getEffectivePodShares plus its getAssetUnstaking(ETH_TOKEN) (split between ethLyingInNDCs / ethStakedInEigenLayer / ethUnstakingFromEigenLayer); the LRT_UNSTAKING_VAULT-registered address's balance (ethLyingInUnstakingVault); the LRT_CONVERTER-registered address's ethValueInWithdrawal() (ethLyingInConverter). Pure read; sensitive to every NDC and to whatever lives at LRT_UNSTAKING_VAULT and LRT_CONVERTER on lrtConfig at call time.

getNodeDelegatorQueue.description: Array view returning every address marked isNodeDelegator[ndc] == 1. The index of each entry is the ndcIndex parameter required by transferAssetToNodeDelegator and transferETHToNodeDelegator. Mutated by addNodeDelegatorContractToQueue (DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, bounded by maxNodeDelegatorLimit) and removeNodeDelegatorContractFromQueue / removeManyNodeDelegatorContractsFromQueue (DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, gated by the maxNegligibleAmount dust check). Order is not stable across removals: removal swaps the removed entry with the array tail and pops, so any cached ndcIndex from before a removal can refer to a different NDC after.

entries.name: NodeDelegator_B1
entries.description: Per-instance EigenLayer staker: holds an EigenPod for native-ETH restaking, ERC20 strategy shares for LST restaking, and delegates the resulting stake to a single EigenLayer operator via DelegationManager. Role checks consult IAccessControl on lrtConfig. Role gates: OPERATOR_ROLE: depositAssetIntoStrategy (deposits this contract's full balance of asset into IStrategy(lrtConfig.assetStrategy(asset)) via EIGEN_STRATEGY_MANAGER, recipient is address(this)), stake32Eth and stake32EthValidated (each consume 32 ether of this contract's ETH balance to register a validator via EigenPodManager.stake; withdrawal credentials derived from eigenPod, pubkey deduplicated through PUBKEY_REGISTRY, stakedButUnverifiedNativeETH += 32 ether), verifyWithdrawalCredentials (proves validators against eigenPod, stakedButUnverifiedNativeETH -= 32 ether per validator), startCheckpoint, processClaim (recipient is lrtConfig.eigenLayerRewardReceiver()), initiateUnstaking (withdrawer is address(this), bumps LRT_UNSTAKING_VAULT.uncompletedWithdrawalCount), completeUnstaking (forwards the resulting balance delta to LRT_UNSTAKING_VAULT when receiveAsTokens). MANAGER: delegateTo (caller picks the EigenLayer operator and supplies the operator's approverSignatureAndExpiry), createEigenPod (calls EigenPodManager.createPod and writes eigenPod, reverts if already set), undelegate (auto-queues one forced withdrawal per active strategy), maxApproveToEigenStrategyManager / revokeApprovalToEigenStrategyManager. ASSET_TRANSFER_ROLE: transferBackToLRTDepositPool, transferETHToLRTUnstakingVault. PAUSER_ROLE: pause. DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE: unpause. Caller-restricted: sendETHFromDepositPoolToNDC requires msg.sender == LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL, sendETHFromUnstakingVaultToNDC requires msg.sender == LRT_UNSTAKING_VAULT, receive() accepts ETH from any caller. eigenPod is also written lazily by stake32Eth on the first call when it is still zero (read from EigenPodManager.ownerToPod). whenNotPaused gates only depositAssetIntoStrategy, stake32Eth, processClaim, undelegate, initiateUnstaking, completeUnstaking, transferBackToLRTDepositPool and transferETHToLRTUnstakingVault; delegateTo, createEigenPod, verifyWithdrawalCredentials, startCheckpoint, the approve helpers, pause and unpause execute regardless of paused state.

lrtConfig.description: Address of the LRTConfig used for every role check on this contract (onlyLRTAdmin, onlyLRTManager, onlyLRTOperator, onlyAssetTransferRole, onlyRole(PAUSER_ROLE)) and every registry lookup (getContract for EIGEN_STRATEGY_MANAGER, EIGEN_DELEGATION_MANAGER, EIGEN_POD_MANAGER, EIGEN_REWARDS_COORDINATOR, BEACON_CHAIN_ETH_STRATEGY, LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL, LRT_UNSTAKING_VAULT, PUBKEY_REGISTRY, plus assetStrategy(asset), eigenLayerRewardReceiver(), and isSupportedAsset(asset) reads). Set in initialize and not updateable by any function on this contract.

paused.description: Inherited PausableUpgradeable state. Set true by pause() (PAUSER_ROLE on lrtConfig) and false by unpause() (DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE on lrtConfig). Blocks only the whenNotPaused-gated subset of functions (depositAssetIntoStrategy, stake32Eth, processClaim, undelegate, initiateUnstaking, completeUnstaking, transferBackToLRTDepositPool, transferETHToLRTUnstakingVault); MANAGER's delegateTo / createEigenPod / approve helpers and OPERATOR's verifyWithdrawalCredentials / startCheckpoint continue to execute while paused.

eigenPod.description: EigenPod owned by this NodeDelegator and used for native-ETH restaking. Written by createEigenPod (MANAGER, reverts if already set) or lazily by stake32Eth (OPERATOR, when still zero, by reading EigenPodManager.ownerToPod(address(this))). Once set, the field is not cleared by any function on this contract. Used as the verify / checkpoint target by verifyWithdrawalCredentials and startCheckpoint, and as the implicit destination of validator beacon-chain withdrawals (the validator's withdrawal credentials are determined by the EigenPod, not by callers of stake32Eth).

elOperatorDelegatedTo.description: EigenLayer operator this NodeDelegator's restaked stake is currently delegated to. Not a stored value; resolved live by calling EIGEN_DELEGATION_MANAGER.delegatedTo(address(this)). Changed only by MANAGER, via delegateTo (sets it to a caller-supplied operator that has approved this delegation, either by having no approver set or by signing the supplied approverSignatureAndExpiry) or undelegate (clears it and queues one forced withdrawal per active strategy).

stakedButUnverifiedNativeETH.description: Running total (in wei) of ETH staked into beacon-chain validators via stake32Eth whose withdrawal credentials have not yet been proven against the EigenPod. Incremented by exactly 32 ether on each successful stake32Eth and decremented by 32 ether per validator on each successful verifyWithdrawalCredentials. Read by getEffectivePodShares as part of the ETH backing this NDC reports to the rest of the protocol; an unproven-or-stuck validator therefore keeps inflating this counter and the reported pod shares until verifyWithdrawalCredentials lands.

getEffectivePodShares.description: View returning stakedButUnverifiedNativeETH plus the withdrawable share read from IStrategy(lrtConfig.beaconChainETHStrategy()) for this contract. Represents the ETH-equivalent native-restaking position this NDC contributes to the protocol-wide TVL. The withdrawable-share component is read from EigenLayer state and reflects beacon-chain balance changes (rewards, slashing, exits) at call time.

entries.name: AllocationManagerView
entries.description: Read-only view contract that exposes query functions for the AllocationManager, allowing external callers to look up operator stake allocations, magnitudes, operator sets, and slashable/redistributable status.

entries.name: UpgradeableBeacon
entries.description: UpgradeableBeacon managing the single implementation for all strategies deployed via StrategyFactory.

entries.name: UpgradeableBeacon
entries.description: A beacon with an upgradeable implementation currently set as eth:0xC355123d0a51b4B5185aA7f21150904CEE3EAC97. Beacon proxy contracts pointing to this beacon will all use its implementation.

entries.name: AVSDirectory
entries.description: AVSDirectory: legacy EigenLayer (operator, AVS) registration registry. The override below adds a kelp-scoped derived field that walks every AVS Kelp's two operators (ELOperator_A, ELOperator_B) are currently registered with on this directory, so the BFS pulls their internals (ServiceManager, registries, slasher chain) into this discovery automatically. List refreshes on every discovery run; the only deployment-stable inputs hardcoded in the where clauses are the two operator addresses (which are also named in this project and would surface as a separate diff if MANAGER ever undelegates and re-delegates).

kelpOperatorAvsRegistrations.description: Per-operator set of AVS addresses each Kelp NodeDelegator-delegated EigenLayer operator is currently REGISTERED with on this AVSDirectory. Reconstructed live from OperatorAVSRegistrationStatusUpdated events restricted to Kelp's two operators; entries appear when the operator's status flips to REGISTERED (1) and disappear when it flips to DEREGISTERED (0). The address values are walked by the BFS so each AVS's middleware stack is pulled into this discovery; the (operator) keys are not walked.

entries.name: KelpInstantWithdrawalFeeRecipient
entries.description: KelpInstantWithdrawalFeeRecipient — set as LRTWithdrawalManager.instantWithdrawalFeeRecipient. Collects the fee users pay (in WETH) for taking the instant-withdrawal path instead of the standard EigenLayer-cooldown queue. Does not mint or burn rsETH directly, but the instant path always burns rsETH against Aave-backed WETH liquidity, so this multisig is the economic counterparty for every instant rsETH redemption.

entries.name: PermissionController
entries.description: Contract that enables AVSs and operators to delegate the ability to call certain core contract functions to other addresses.

entries.name: ProtocolRegistry
entries.description: Admin-controlled on-chain registry that tracks all EigenLayer protocol contract deployments (addresses, names, configs, and versioning) and provides a pauseAll function to pause every registered pausable contract in the protocol.

entries.name: KelpProtocolTreasury
entries.description: KelpProtocolTreasury — registered under PROTOCOL_TREASURY in LRTConfig. Receives the rsETH that LRTOracle.updateRSETHPrice() mints as the protocol fee on every rebase (sized by protocolFeeInBPS, capped by the daily fee-mint limit). This multisig holds the realized rsETH protocol revenue.

entries.name: LRTOracle
entries.description: Computes the rsETH/ETH exchange rate (rsETHPrice) on demand and mints the protocol fee. Two entrypoints: updateRSETHPrice is callable by anyone when this contract is not paused; updateRSETHPriceAsManager is callable by MANAGER and ignores both the paused gate and the upper-bound percentage check. Each successful update sums per-asset getTotalAssetDeposits read from the LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL-registered address times the per-asset assetPriceOracle reading, takes protocolFeeInBPS / 10000 of any TVL increase, mints that as rsETH to the PROTOCOL_TREASURY-registered address (capped per 24h by maxFeeMintAmountPerDay, skipped while this contract or the LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL or LRT_WITHDRAW_MANAGER address is paused), then writes rsETHPrice = (totalETHInProtocol - fee) / rsETH.totalSupply. Two non-obvious behaviours: a price increase exceeding pricePercentageLimit reverts the public path (only updateRSETHPriceAsManager can push it through), and a price decrease exceeding pricePercentageLimit auto-pauses this contract and calls pause() on the LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL- and LRT_WITHDRAW_MANAGER-registered addresses. All role checks (onlyLRTAdmin / onlyLRTManager / onlyRole(PAUSER_ROLE)) consult the IAccessControl interface on the address held in lrtConfig.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: fan pause() out to the addresses under LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL, LRT_WITHDRAW_MANAGER, LRT_ORACLE, the rsETH address, and every NodeDelegator returned by getNodeDelegatorQueue(), via pauseAll.

lrtConfig.description: Address of the LRTConfig used for every role check on this contract (onlyLRTAdmin, onlyLRTManager, onlyRole(PAUSER_ROLE)) and every registry lookup (getContract for LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL, LRT_WITHDRAW_MANAGER and PROTOCOL_TREASURY, plus getSupportedAssetList in _updateRsETHPrice). Set in initialize and not updateable by any function on this contract.

rsETHPrice.description: Current rsETH/ETH exchange rate in 1e18 precision (1e18 == 1.0 ETH per rsETH). Writeable only by _updateRsETHPrice, reachable through updateRSETHPrice (public, blocked while paused) or updateRSETHPriceAsManager (MANAGER, bypasses paused). When rsETH.totalSupply == 0, _updateRsETHPrice short-circuits and writes 1 ether without further computation.

highestRsethPrice.description: All-time-high rsETH price observed by _updateRsETHPrice (1e18 precision). Auto-updated whenever a new price exceeds it. Used as the reference value that pricePercentageLimit is multiplied against to derive both the up-revert threshold and the down-pause threshold inside _updateRsETHPrice. No setter; advanced only by the price-update path.

pricePercentageLimit.description: Threshold in 1e18 precision (1e18 == 100%, 1e16 == 1%) controlling _updateRsETHPrice's tolerance for large moves vs highestRsethPrice. If the new price exceeds highestRsethPrice + (limit * highestRsethPrice / 1e18), public updateRSETHPrice reverts and only updateRSETHPriceAsManager (MANAGER) can push through. If the new price falls below highestRsethPrice - (limit * highestRsethPrice / 1e18), _updateRsETHPrice auto-pauses this contract and calls pause() on the LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL- and LRT_WITHDRAW_MANAGER-registered addresses. A value of 0 disables both checks. Writeable only by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE on lrtConfig via setPricePercentageLimit.

maxFeeMintAmountPerDay.description: Cap (in rsETH wei) on the total protocol fee that _updateRsETHPrice can mint within a 24h window anchored on feePeriodStartTime. _updateRsETHPrice reverts if the period's accumulated mint plus the next fee would exceed this. A value of 0 effectively disables fee minting (remainingDailyFeeMintLimit returns 0). Writeable only by MANAGER on lrtConfig via setMaxFeeMintAmountPerDay.

currentPeriodMintedFeeAmount.description: Running total (in rsETH wei) of protocol fee minted by _updateRsETHPrice within the current 24h period. Auto-incremented on each successful fee mint and auto-reset to 0 by _checkAndUpdateDailyFeeMintLimit when block.timestamp crosses feePeriodStartTime + 1 day. No external setter.

feePeriodStartTime.description: Anchor timestamp from which the 24h fee-mint windows are measured. Initial value set by the one-shot reinitialize (reinitializer(2), MANAGER), which requires the supplied timestamp to be no older than 24h and not in the future. Subsequently auto-rolled forward by _checkAndUpdateDailyFeeMintLimit (it is set to getCurrentPeriodStartTime() each time a new day begins).

getCurrentPeriodStartTime.description: View getter returning feePeriodStartTime + floor((block.timestamp - feePeriodStartTime) / 1 day) * 1 day, i.e. the aligned start of the 24h fee-mint period that contains block.timestamp.

getNextDailyLimitResetTimestamp.description: View getter returning getCurrentPeriodStartTime() + 1 day. The earliest block.timestamp at which currentPeriodMintedFeeAmount is treated as reset and the full maxFeeMintAmountPerDay becomes available again.

remainingDailyFeeMintLimit.description: View getter returning max(maxFeeMintAmountPerDay - effectiveMinted, 0), where effectiveMinted is currentPeriodMintedFeeAmount during the active period and 0 once block.timestamp >= feePeriodStartTime + 1 day. Returns 0 whenever maxFeeMintAmountPerDay is 0.

paused.description: Boolean state. Set true by pause() (gated to PAUSER_ROLE on lrtConfig, only when not already paused) and by _updateRsETHPrice itself when a price decrease exceeds pricePercentageLimit. Set false by unpause() (gated to DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE on lrtConfig). While true, the public updateRSETHPrice reverts; updateRSETHPriceAsManager (MANAGER) can still execute and re-set the price.

assetPriceOracles.description: Per-asset value of the assetPriceOracle[asset] mapping, the address whose getAssetPrice(asset) reading _updateRsETHPrice multiplies by the per-asset getTotalAssetDeposits to compute the asset's contribution to totalETHInProtocol. Writeable only by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE on lrtConfig via updatePriceOracleFor (no sanity check) or updatePriceOracleForValidated (which additionally requires the resulting price to lie between 1e16 and 1e19). The mapping is not zeroed by any code path on this contract, so historical entries persist for assets that have been removed from the supported-asset list at lrtConfig.

entries.name: PubkeyRegistry
entries.description: Stores the BLS pubkeys used by NodeDelegators when registering validators with EigenLayer's EigenPodManager.

entries.name: DelegationManager
entries.description: The DelegationManager contract is responsible for registering EigenLayer operators and managing the EigenLayer strategies delegations. The EigenDA StakeRegistry contract reads from the DelegationManager to track the total stake of each EigenDA operator.

entries.name: EthXPriceOracle
entries.description: IPriceFetcher implementation for one specific LST. getAssetPrice reverts unless the supplied asset equals ethxAddress; otherwise it returns IETHXStakePoolsManager(ethXStakePoolsManagerProxyAddress).getExchangeRate() as the asset/ETH rate. Both ethXStakePoolsManagerProxyAddress and ethxAddress are set by one-shot initializers (initialize and initialize2) and have no setters; the implementation behind the proxy is upgradeable through the proxy admin.

ethxAddress.description: The only asset that getAssetPrice will price (it reverts on any other input). Set once in initialize2 by reading getETHxToken() on the StaderConfig pointed to by IETHXStakePoolsManager(ethXStakePoolsManagerProxyAddress).staderConfig(); has no setter.

ethXStakePoolsManagerProxyAddress.description: Address whose getExchangeRate() return value getAssetPrice forwards as the asset/ETH rate. Set once in initialize and not updateable by any function on this contract. Walks into the foreign Stader stack and is therefore listed in ignoreRelatives so the BFS does not follow it.

receivedPermissions.description: executes scheduled operations after the delay

entries.name: TimelockController
entries.description: OZ TimelockController. PROPOSER queues an arbitrary call (target, value, data, predecessor, salt) via schedule / scheduleBatch; once getMinDelay seconds have elapsed, anyone holding EXECUTOR_ROLE (or any address, see below) can run it via execute / executeBatch. CANCELLER aborts a still-pending operation via cancel(id). Two non-obvious patterns to look for in the values: (1) execute / executeBatch are gated by the onlyRoleOrOpenRole(EXECUTOR_ROLE) modifier, which checks address(0) first: if address(0) holds EXECUTOR_ROLE the per-msg.sender check is skipped and execution is permissionless. (2) The constructor grants TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE (the role admin of every other role on this contract) to the contract itself, so role changes can be performed only by scheduling them and waiting the current delay; the constructor also grants the role to its admin argument when non-zero, and OZ recommends renouncing that admin immediately after deployment. updateDelay requires msg.sender == address(this), so changing getMinDelay must itself be scheduled and survive the current delay.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: register a new deposit asset via addNewSupportedAsset.

accessControl.description: Role-by-role gates on this contract. PROPOSER_ROLE: schedule, scheduleBatch. EXECUTOR_ROLE: execute, executeBatch (gated by onlyRoleOrOpenRole, so granting the role to address(0) makes those functions callable by anyone). CANCELLER_ROLE: cancel(id) on any pending operation. TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE: grantRole / revokeRole on every role here (the constructor sets it as the role admin of every role, including itself). Every role's admin is TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE, so role changes can only be performed by holders of TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE (which is granted to the contract itself, so changes normally flow through schedule + delay).

proposers.description: Holders of PROPOSER_ROLE. Sole authority to queue a call via schedule(target, value, data, predecessor, salt, delay) or scheduleBatch; the operation's id is hashOperation(...) of the same parameters, and it becomes executable after at least the supplied delay (which must be >= getMinDelay). Targets, values and data are unconstrained: any contract call can be queued. Membership is changed by TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE holders.

executors.description: Holders of EXECUTOR_ROLE. execute and executeBatch are gated by onlyRoleOrOpenRole(EXECUTOR_ROLE), which checks address(0) first: if address(0) appears in this list, the per-msg.sender check is skipped and any address can execute a queued operation once its delay has elapsed. The address(0) entry is therefore the OZ open-role signal, not a real holder. While address(0) is present, additional grants here are inert (the open-role short-circuit fires first); revoking the role from address(0) is what closes the role to non-members. Executing does not let the caller pick what runs: the call payload was committed at schedule time. Membership is changed by TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE holders.

cancellers.description: Holders of CANCELLER_ROLE. Can call cancel(id) at any time between schedule and execution to abort a pending operation; cancel reverts on already-executed operations. Membership is changed by TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE holders.

timelockAdmins.description: Holders of TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE. The constructor sets this role as the role admin of TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE, PROPOSER_ROLE, EXECUTOR_ROLE and CANCELLER_ROLE, so members can call grantRole / revokeRole for any of them. The constructor always grants this role to the contract itself, so role changes can be performed as a normal scheduled-then-executed operation, and additionally grants it to the constructor's admin argument when non-zero (the OZ-recommended flow is to renounce that admin immediately after deployment). Any holder here other than the timelock contract itself can grant or revoke roles directly without going through the delay.

getMinDelay.description: Minimum delay in seconds between scheduling an operation and being able to execute it. Changeable only by updateDelay, which requires msg.sender == address(this); a delay change must therefore itself be scheduled by a PROPOSER, wait the current delay, and then be executed.

entries.name: NodeDelegator_A3
entries.description: Per-instance EigenLayer staker: holds an EigenPod for native-ETH restaking, ERC20 strategy shares for LST restaking, and delegates the resulting stake to a single EigenLayer operator via DelegationManager. Role checks consult IAccessControl on lrtConfig. Role gates: OPERATOR_ROLE: depositAssetIntoStrategy (deposits this contract's full balance of asset into IStrategy(lrtConfig.assetStrategy(asset)) via EIGEN_STRATEGY_MANAGER, recipient is address(this)), stake32Eth and stake32EthValidated (each consume 32 ether of this contract's ETH balance to register a validator via EigenPodManager.stake; withdrawal credentials derived from eigenPod, pubkey deduplicated through PUBKEY_REGISTRY, stakedButUnverifiedNativeETH += 32 ether), verifyWithdrawalCredentials (proves validators against eigenPod, stakedButUnverifiedNativeETH -= 32 ether per validator), startCheckpoint, processClaim (recipient is lrtConfig.eigenLayerRewardReceiver()), initiateUnstaking (withdrawer is address(this), bumps LRT_UNSTAKING_VAULT.uncompletedWithdrawalCount), completeUnstaking (forwards the resulting balance delta to LRT_UNSTAKING_VAULT when receiveAsTokens). MANAGER: delegateTo (caller picks the EigenLayer operator and supplies the operator's approverSignatureAndExpiry), createEigenPod (calls EigenPodManager.createPod and writes eigenPod, reverts if already set), undelegate (auto-queues one forced withdrawal per active strategy), maxApproveToEigenStrategyManager / revokeApprovalToEigenStrategyManager. ASSET_TRANSFER_ROLE: transferBackToLRTDepositPool, transferETHToLRTUnstakingVault. PAUSER_ROLE: pause. DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE: unpause. Caller-restricted: sendETHFromDepositPoolToNDC requires msg.sender == LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL, sendETHFromUnstakingVaultToNDC requires msg.sender == LRT_UNSTAKING_VAULT, receive() accepts ETH from any caller. eigenPod is also written lazily by stake32Eth on the first call when it is still zero (read from EigenPodManager.ownerToPod). whenNotPaused gates only depositAssetIntoStrategy, stake32Eth, processClaim, undelegate, initiateUnstaking, completeUnstaking, transferBackToLRTDepositPool and transferETHToLRTUnstakingVault; delegateTo, createEigenPod, verifyWithdrawalCredentials, startCheckpoint, the approve helpers, pause and unpause execute regardless of paused state.

lrtConfig.description: Address of the LRTConfig used for every role check on this contract (onlyLRTAdmin, onlyLRTManager, onlyLRTOperator, onlyAssetTransferRole, onlyRole(PAUSER_ROLE)) and every registry lookup (getContract for EIGEN_STRATEGY_MANAGER, EIGEN_DELEGATION_MANAGER, EIGEN_POD_MANAGER, EIGEN_REWARDS_COORDINATOR, BEACON_CHAIN_ETH_STRATEGY, LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL, LRT_UNSTAKING_VAULT, PUBKEY_REGISTRY, plus assetStrategy(asset), eigenLayerRewardReceiver(), and isSupportedAsset(asset) reads). Set in initialize and not updateable by any function on this contract.

paused.description: Inherited PausableUpgradeable state. Set true by pause() (PAUSER_ROLE on lrtConfig) and false by unpause() (DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE on lrtConfig). Blocks only the whenNotPaused-gated subset of functions (depositAssetIntoStrategy, stake32Eth, processClaim, undelegate, initiateUnstaking, completeUnstaking, transferBackToLRTDepositPool, transferETHToLRTUnstakingVault); MANAGER's delegateTo / createEigenPod / approve helpers and OPERATOR's verifyWithdrawalCredentials / startCheckpoint continue to execute while paused.

eigenPod.description: EigenPod owned by this NodeDelegator and used for native-ETH restaking. Written by createEigenPod (MANAGER, reverts if already set) or lazily by stake32Eth (OPERATOR, when still zero, by reading EigenPodManager.ownerToPod(address(this))). Once set, the field is not cleared by any function on this contract. Used as the verify / checkpoint target by verifyWithdrawalCredentials and startCheckpoint, and as the implicit destination of validator beacon-chain withdrawals (the validator's withdrawal credentials are determined by the EigenPod, not by callers of stake32Eth).

elOperatorDelegatedTo.description: EigenLayer operator this NodeDelegator's restaked stake is currently delegated to. Not a stored value; resolved live by calling EIGEN_DELEGATION_MANAGER.delegatedTo(address(this)). Changed only by MANAGER, via delegateTo (sets it to a caller-supplied operator that has approved this delegation, either by having no approver set or by signing the supplied approverSignatureAndExpiry) or undelegate (clears it and queues one forced withdrawal per active strategy).

stakedButUnverifiedNativeETH.description: Running total (in wei) of ETH staked into beacon-chain validators via stake32Eth whose withdrawal credentials have not yet been proven against the EigenPod. Incremented by exactly 32 ether on each successful stake32Eth and decremented by 32 ether per validator on each successful verifyWithdrawalCredentials. Read by getEffectivePodShares as part of the ETH backing this NDC reports to the rest of the protocol; an unproven-or-stuck validator therefore keeps inflating this counter and the reported pod shares until verifyWithdrawalCredentials lands.

getEffectivePodShares.description: View returning stakedButUnverifiedNativeETH plus the withdrawable share read from IStrategy(lrtConfig.beaconChainETHStrategy()) for this contract. Represents the ETH-equivalent native-restaking position this NDC contributes to the protocol-wide TVL. The withdrawable-share component is read from EigenLayer state and reflects beacon-chain balance changes (rewards, slashing, exits) at call time.

entries.name: OneETHPriceOracle
entries.description: IPriceFetcher implementation that returns 1e18 (asset/ETH = 1.0) from getAssetPrice regardless of which asset address is passed (the argument is ignored). Pure function, no state, no admin surface, deployed as a plain non-proxy contract.

entries.name: KelpAssetTransferMultisig
entries.description: KelpAssetTransferMultisig — holds ASSET_TRANSFER_ROLE on LRTConfig. Can move pooled LSTs/ETH between LRTDepositPool, NodeDelegators and LRTUnstakingVault. Does not change rsETH supply, but controls the assets that back rsETH at any given moment, so it can rebalance Kelp's restaked vs idle balance.

receivedPermissions.description: fan pause() out to the addresses under LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL, LRT_WITHDRAW_MANAGER, LRT_ORACLE, the rsETH address, and every NodeDelegator returned by getNodeDelegatorQueue(), via pauseAll.

entries.name: EigenPod
entries.description: EigenLayer EigenPod, one per staker. podOwner is set in initialize when the BeaconProxy is deployed by EigenPodManager and is not changeable afterwards. Validators staked through this pod have withdrawal credentials hardcoded to address(this), so beacon-chain withdrawals always exit into this pod. Function gates: onlyEigenPodManager: stake (msg.value must equal 32 ether, calls ETHPOS.deposit with this pod's withdrawal credentials), withdrawRestakedBeaconChainETH (sends ETH to a manager-supplied recipient, capped at withdrawableRestakedExecutionLayerGwei). onlyEigenPodOwner: recoverTokens (sweeps arbitrary ERC20 tokens to a caller-supplied recipient; does not touch native ETH or restaked balance accounting), setProofSubmitter. onlyOwnerOrProofSubmitter: startCheckpoint, verifyWithdrawalCredentials, requestConsolidation (forwards a fee + pubkey-pair payload to the EIP-7251 consolidation predeploy), requestWithdrawal (forwards a fee + pubkey/amount payload to the EIP-7002 withdrawal predeploy). Permissionless: receive() (accepts ETH from any caller, logs only), verifyCheckpointProofs (any caller advances the active checkpoint with valid Merkle proofs), verifyStaleBalance (any caller can mark a validator with stale balance to require a re-checkpoint). Each non-Manager entrypoint is independently gated by IPausable(eigenPodManager).paused(index) with a function-specific pause index, so EigenLayer's pause governance on EigenPodManager controls each operation separately.

podOwner.description: The pod's owner. Set once by initialize when EigenPodManager deploys this BeaconProxy and not updateable by any function on this contract. Powers on this contract: recoverTokens (sweep arbitrary ERC20 tokens lying on this contract to a caller-supplied recipient), setProofSubmitter (set the proofSubmitter address), startCheckpoint, verifyWithdrawalCredentials, requestConsolidation (EIP-7251 consolidation predeploy), requestWithdrawal (EIP-7002 withdrawal predeploy, can request a partial or full beacon-chain validator exit but the resulting ETH lands in this pod). Cannot withdraw the pod's restaked ETH directly: withdrawRestakedBeaconChainETH is onlyEigenPodManager and chooses the recipient itself.

proofSubmitter.description: Optional second address allowed to call the onlyOwnerOrProofSubmitter subset: startCheckpoint, verifyWithdrawalCredentials, requestConsolidation, requestWithdrawal. Cannot recoverTokens, cannot setProofSubmitter. Settable only by podOwner via setProofSubmitter; defaults to address(0), which makes those four entrypoints podOwner-only.

eigenPodManager.description: Immutable, set in the constructor. The single EigenLayer EigenPodManager: sole caller permitted to invoke stake() and withdrawRestakedBeaconChainETH(), and the source of pause status read by every IPausable(eigenPodManager).paused(index) check.

ethPOS.description: Immutable, set in the constructor. Beacon-chain deposit contract that stake() forwards 32 ether to with this pod's withdrawal credentials.

activeValidatorCount.description: Number of validators currently in VALIDATOR_STATUS.ACTIVE. Incremented in verifyWithdrawalCredentials when a new validator's credentials are proven against this pod; decremented (validator transitions to WITHDRAWN) inside verifyCheckpointProofs when a proof shows balance == 0.

currentCheckpoint.description: In-progress checkpoint state. proofsRemaining counts ACTIVE validators not yet checkpointed; podBalanceGwei pins the pod's execution-layer ETH balance at checkpoint start; balanceDeltasGwei accumulates per-validator balance changes proven during the checkpoint; prevBeaconBalanceGwei accumulates each ACTIVE validator's last-known balance. Active iff currentCheckpointTimestamp != 0; cleared when proofsRemaining reaches 0 and the checkpoint finalizes.

currentCheckpointTimestamp.description: Beacon timestamp the active checkpoint was pinned against, or 0 if no checkpoint is active. Set by startCheckpoint, cleared on finalization. While non-zero, verifyWithdrawalCredentials calls require beaconTimestamp > currentCheckpointTimestamp.

lastCheckpointTimestamp.description: Beacon timestamp of the most recently finalized checkpoint. Used as the lower bound for verifyWithdrawalCredentials (beaconTimestamp must exceed it) and as the default lastCheckpointedAt for newly-proven validators when no checkpoint is currently active.

withdrawableRestakedExecutionLayerGwei.description: View returning the internal restakedExecutionLayerGwei: ETH (in gwei) sitting in this pod that has been moved from the beacon chain to the execution layer via finalized checkpoints / exits. Available for withdrawRestakedBeaconChainETH (callable only by eigenPodManager) up to this amount.

getConsolidationRequestFee.description: Live read of the EIP-7251 consolidation predeploy's current fee. Updated per-block by the consensus layer; quoted by requestConsolidation.

getWithdrawalRequestFee.description: Live read of the EIP-7002 withdrawal predeploy's current fee. Updated per-block by the consensus layer; quoted by requestWithdrawal.

entries.name: AaveProtocolDataProvider
entries.description: Aave V3 read-only data provider. Lists every reserve and aToken in the Aave V3 main pool.

entries.name: SfrxETHPriceOracle
entries.description: IPriceFetcher implementation for one specific LST. getAssetPrice reverts unless the supplied asset equals sfrxETHContractAddress; otherwise it returns ISfrxETH(sfrxETHContractAddress).pricePerShare() as the asset/ETH rate. sfrxETHContractAddress is set once in initialize and has no setter; the implementation behind the proxy is upgradeable through the proxy admin.

sfrxETHContractAddress.description: Address whose pricePerShare() return value getAssetPrice forwards as the asset/ETH rate, and the only asset address that getAssetPrice will accept (it reverts on any other input). Set once in initialize and not updateable by any function on this contract. Walks into the foreign Frax stack and is therefore listed in ignoreRelatives so the BFS does not follow it.

entries.name: StrategyManager
entries.description: The StrategyManager contract is responsible for managing the EigenLayer token strategies. Each EigenDA quorum has at least one strategy that defines the operators quorum stake.

entries.name: LRTConfig
entries.description: OZ AccessControl + bytes32-keyed configuration store designed to be shared by a multi-contract protocol. Sibling contracts read role membership via IAccessControl and look up sibling addresses via getContract(KEY) / getLSTToken(KEY). The only state-changing function on this contract that consumes the registry is pauseAll, which fans pause() out to the addresses under LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL, LRT_WITHDRAW_MANAGER and LRT_ORACLE, to the rsETH address, and to every NodeDelegator returned by getNodeDelegatorQueue(). Five of the role identifiers declared in the embedded LRTConstants library (MINTER_ROLE, BURNER_ROLE, OPERATOR_ROLE, ASSET_TRANSFER_ROLE, UNLOCKED_WITHDRAWAL_INITIALIZER) are not gated by any function on this contract; they exist only for sibling contracts to query.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: fan pause() out to the addresses under LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL, LRT_WITHDRAW_MANAGER, LRT_ORACLE, the rsETH address, and every NodeDelegator returned by getNodeDelegatorQueue(), via pauseAll.

accessControl.description: Role-by-role gates on functions in this contract. DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE: setContract, setToken, setRSETH, setEigenLayerRewardReceiver, setMaxNegligibleAmount, removeSupportedAsset (reverts unless the asset's getTotalAssetDeposits at the LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL-registered address is at or below maxNegligibleAmount), updateAssetStrategy (reverts if any entry returned by getNodeDelegatorQueue() has non-zero shares in the previous strategy), plus the OZ-default grantRole / revokeRole of every other role. MANAGER: setProtocolFeeBps (reverts if value > 1500, i.e. 15%), updateAssetDepositLimit (any already-supported asset). PAUSER_ROLE: pauseAll only. TIME_LOCK_ROLE: addNewSupportedAsset only (and the depositLimit > 0 invariant is enforced by the setter).

defaultAdmins.description: Holders of the root admin role. Powers on this contract: setContract, setToken, setRSETH, setEigenLayerRewardReceiver, setMaxNegligibleAmount, removeSupportedAsset (subject to the maxNegligibleAmount dust gate against ILRTDepositPool.getTotalAssetDeposits), updateAssetStrategy (subject to the no-shares-in-prev-strategy check across the NodeDelegator queue), and the OZ-default grantRole / revokeRole of every role on this contract (since DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE is the role admin of every role here). Membership is changed by current DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE holders only.

managers.description: Holders of the operational-config role. Powers on this contract: setProtocolFeeBps (caps protocolFeeInBPS at 1500, i.e. 15%) and updateAssetDepositLimit (any already-supported asset). Cannot grant or revoke roles, swap registry entries, or pause anything. Membership is changed by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE holders.

pausers.description: Holders of the circuit-breaker role. The only function on this contract gated to this role is pauseAll, which iterates over the LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL / LRT_WITHDRAW_MANAGER / LRT_ORACLE addresses, the rsETH address, and every NodeDelegator returned by getNodeDelegatorQueue(), and calls IPausable(...).pause() on each (skipping any target already paused). There is no unpause function on this contract. Membership is changed by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE holders.

minters.description: Holders of MINTER_ROLE. Not consumed by any function on this contract; the role identifier is declared in the embedded LRTConstants library so sibling contracts can read membership via IAccessControl. Membership is changed by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE holders on this contract.

burners.description: Holders of BURNER_ROLE. Not consumed by any function on this contract; the role identifier is declared in the embedded LRTConstants library so sibling contracts can read membership via IAccessControl. Membership is changed by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE holders on this contract.

operators.description: Holders of OPERATOR_ROLE. Not consumed by any function on this contract; the role identifier is declared in the embedded LRTConstants library so sibling contracts can read membership via IAccessControl. Membership is changed by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE holders on this contract.

assetTransferRole.description: Holders of ASSET_TRANSFER_ROLE. Not consumed by any function on this contract; the role identifier is declared in the embedded LRTConstants library so sibling contracts can read membership via IAccessControl. Membership is changed by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE holders on this contract.

timelockRole.description: Holders of TIME_LOCK_ROLE. The only function on this contract gated to this role is addNewSupportedAsset, which appends an asset to supportedAssetList, marks it isSupportedAsset, and writes its initial depositLimitByAsset (with a depositLimit > 0 invariant). Membership is changed by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE holders.

unlockedWithdrawalInitializers.description: Holders of UNLOCKED_WITHDRAWAL_INITIALIZER role. Not consumed by any function on this contract; the role identifier is declared in the embedded LRTConstants library so sibling contracts can read membership via IAccessControl. Membership is changed by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE holders on this contract.

registeredContracts.description: bytes32-keyed contract registry (contractMap). Writeable only by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE via setContract. Read on this contract only by pauseAll (LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL, LRT_WITHDRAW_MANAGER, LRT_ORACLE keys), removeSupportedAsset (LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL) and updateAssetStrategy (LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL); the other keys are stored solely for sibling contracts to look up via getContract(KEY).

registeredTokens.description: bytes32-keyed token registry (tokenMap). Writeable by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE via setToken; the ST_ETH_TOKEN and ETHX_TOKEN keys are also seeded by the initialize function. No state-changing function on this contract reads from it; sibling contracts look up tokens via the getLSTToken(KEY) view.

eigenLayerRewardReceiver.description: Address stored on this contract and exposed via the public eigenLayerRewardReceiver getter. Writeable only by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE via setEigenLayerRewardReceiver. Not consumed by any function on this contract; sibling contracts read it for their own reward-routing logic.

rsETH.description: Address of the rsETH token contract. Initialized in initialize and replaceable by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE via setRSETH (a dedicated setter, separate from the bytes32 contract registry). Read on this contract only by pauseAll, which calls IPausable(rsETH).pause().

supportedAssetList.description: Array of deposit assets accepted by the protocol, mirrored by isSupportedAsset[asset]. Native ETH is represented by the LRTConstants.ETH_TOKEN sentinel (0xEeee...EEeE). Appended by addNewSupportedAsset (TIME_LOCK_ROLE). Removed by removeSupportedAsset (DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE), which reverts unless the asset's getTotalAssetDeposits at the LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL-registered address is at or below maxNegligibleAmount. The onlySupportedAsset modifier (used on updateAssetDepositLimit, updateAssetStrategy and removeSupportedAsset) checks against this list.

getSupportedAssetList.description: View getter that returns the entire supportedAssetList in one call. Same content as supportedAssetList; provided so callers can fetch the full list without iterating the indexed array getter.

protocolFeeInBPS.description: Protocol fee in basis points (1 = 0.01%). Writeable only by MANAGER via setProtocolFeeBps, which reverts if the new value exceeds 1500 (15%). Not consumed by any function on this contract; sibling contracts read it via the public protocolFeeInBPS getter.

maxNegligibleAmount.description: Dust threshold in 18-decimal asset units. Writeable only by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE via setMaxNegligibleAmount. On this contract it gates removeSupportedAsset, which reverts unless the asset's getTotalAssetDeposits reading from the LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL-registered address is at or below this value. Sibling contracts read the public getter for their own dust-tolerance checks.

assetStrategies.description: Per-asset value of the assetStrategy[asset] mapping. Writeable only by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE via updateAssetStrategy, which reverts if any entry returned by getNodeDelegatorQueue() on the LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL-registered address has non-zero shares in the previous strategy. Removed assets ghost: removeSupportedAsset zeroes assetStrategy[asset] in storage but emits no AssetStrategyUpdate event, so an asset that was once supported keeps its last-seen strategy in this view; cross-reference supportedAssetList for the currently-active set.

assetDepositLimits.description: Per-asset value of the depositLimitByAsset[asset] mapping (in 18-decimal asset units). Initial value set by addNewSupportedAsset (TIME_LOCK_ROLE, with a depositLimit > 0 invariant). Updates by updateAssetDepositLimit (MANAGER, requires onlySupportedAsset). Removed assets ghost: removeSupportedAsset zeroes the storage entry but emits no AssetDepositLimitUpdate event, so removed assets keep their last-seen limit in this view; cross-reference supportedAssetList for the currently-active set.

entries.name: AllocationManager

entries.name: StrategyBaseTVLLimits
entries.description: A strategy implementation allowing to deposit a specific token as a restakable asset.

values.explanation: Base Strategy implementation to inherit from for more complex implementations

maxPerDeposit.description: Maximum value of one deposit transaction

entries.name: rsETH Token
entries.description: rsETH liquid restaking token. ERC20 minted on deposits to LRTDepositPool and burned on withdrawals via LRTWithdrawalManager. ERC20Permit + AccessControl + Pausable with a per-block transfer guard.

entries.name: ETHx Token
entries.description: Stader ETHx — accepted as a deposit asset by KelpDAO. Shown as a leaf: ERC20 metadata is captured but Stader's StaderConfig chain is not walked.

entries.name: EigenPod
entries.description: EigenLayer EigenPod, one per staker. podOwner is set in initialize when the BeaconProxy is deployed by EigenPodManager and is not changeable afterwards. Validators staked through this pod have withdrawal credentials hardcoded to address(this), so beacon-chain withdrawals always exit into this pod. Function gates: onlyEigenPodManager: stake (msg.value must equal 32 ether, calls ETHPOS.deposit with this pod's withdrawal credentials), withdrawRestakedBeaconChainETH (sends ETH to a manager-supplied recipient, capped at withdrawableRestakedExecutionLayerGwei). onlyEigenPodOwner: recoverTokens (sweeps arbitrary ERC20 tokens to a caller-supplied recipient; does not touch native ETH or restaked balance accounting), setProofSubmitter. onlyOwnerOrProofSubmitter: startCheckpoint, verifyWithdrawalCredentials, requestConsolidation (forwards a fee + pubkey-pair payload to the EIP-7251 consolidation predeploy), requestWithdrawal (forwards a fee + pubkey/amount payload to the EIP-7002 withdrawal predeploy). Permissionless: receive() (accepts ETH from any caller, logs only), verifyCheckpointProofs (any caller advances the active checkpoint with valid Merkle proofs), verifyStaleBalance (any caller can mark a validator with stale balance to require a re-checkpoint). Each non-Manager entrypoint is independently gated by IPausable(eigenPodManager).paused(index) with a function-specific pause index, so EigenLayer's pause governance on EigenPodManager controls each operation separately.

podOwner.description: The pod's owner. Set once by initialize when EigenPodManager deploys this BeaconProxy and not updateable by any function on this contract. Powers on this contract: recoverTokens (sweep arbitrary ERC20 tokens lying on this contract to a caller-supplied recipient), setProofSubmitter (set the proofSubmitter address), startCheckpoint, verifyWithdrawalCredentials, requestConsolidation (EIP-7251 consolidation predeploy), requestWithdrawal (EIP-7002 withdrawal predeploy, can request a partial or full beacon-chain validator exit but the resulting ETH lands in this pod). Cannot withdraw the pod's restaked ETH directly: withdrawRestakedBeaconChainETH is onlyEigenPodManager and chooses the recipient itself.

proofSubmitter.description: Optional second address allowed to call the onlyOwnerOrProofSubmitter subset: startCheckpoint, verifyWithdrawalCredentials, requestConsolidation, requestWithdrawal. Cannot recoverTokens, cannot setProofSubmitter. Settable only by podOwner via setProofSubmitter; defaults to address(0), which makes those four entrypoints podOwner-only.

eigenPodManager.description: Immutable, set in the constructor. The single EigenLayer EigenPodManager: sole caller permitted to invoke stake() and withdrawRestakedBeaconChainETH(), and the source of pause status read by every IPausable(eigenPodManager).paused(index) check.

ethPOS.description: Immutable, set in the constructor. Beacon-chain deposit contract that stake() forwards 32 ether to with this pod's withdrawal credentials.

activeValidatorCount.description: Number of validators currently in VALIDATOR_STATUS.ACTIVE. Incremented in verifyWithdrawalCredentials when a new validator's credentials are proven against this pod; decremented (validator transitions to WITHDRAWN) inside verifyCheckpointProofs when a proof shows balance == 0.

currentCheckpoint.description: In-progress checkpoint state. proofsRemaining counts ACTIVE validators not yet checkpointed; podBalanceGwei pins the pod's execution-layer ETH balance at checkpoint start; balanceDeltasGwei accumulates per-validator balance changes proven during the checkpoint; prevBeaconBalanceGwei accumulates each ACTIVE validator's last-known balance. Active iff currentCheckpointTimestamp != 0; cleared when proofsRemaining reaches 0 and the checkpoint finalizes.

currentCheckpointTimestamp.description: Beacon timestamp the active checkpoint was pinned against, or 0 if no checkpoint is active. Set by startCheckpoint, cleared on finalization. While non-zero, verifyWithdrawalCredentials calls require beaconTimestamp > currentCheckpointTimestamp.

lastCheckpointTimestamp.description: Beacon timestamp of the most recently finalized checkpoint. Used as the lower bound for verifyWithdrawalCredentials (beaconTimestamp must exceed it) and as the default lastCheckpointedAt for newly-proven validators when no checkpoint is currently active.

withdrawableRestakedExecutionLayerGwei.description: View returning the internal restakedExecutionLayerGwei: ETH (in gwei) sitting in this pod that has been moved from the beacon chain to the execution layer via finalized checkpoints / exits. Available for withdrawRestakedBeaconChainETH (callable only by eigenPodManager) up to this amount.

getConsolidationRequestFee.description: Live read of the EIP-7251 consolidation predeploy's current fee. Updated per-block by the consensus layer; quoted by requestConsolidation.

getWithdrawalRequestFee.description: Live read of the EIP-7002 withdrawal predeploy's current fee. Updated per-block by the consensus layer; quoted by requestWithdrawal.

entries.name: UnlockedWithdrawalsInitializer
entries.description: Holds the UNLOCKED_WITHDRAWAL_INITIALIZER role on LRTConfig. The role gates only LRTWithdrawalManager.initialize2, a one-shot reinitializer that seeds the per-asset unlockedWithdrawalsCount counter from the legacy withdrawal flow. After being called once, further calls are blocked by the OZ reinitializer guard, so this contract is effectively spent on the live deployment and retains no live authority over withdrawals.

entries.name: KelpAdminMultisig
entries.description: KelpAdminMultisig — root admin of the rsETH protocol. Holds DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE on LRTConfig: can grant/revoke any role on every Kelp contract (including MINTER_ROLE and BURNER_ROLE on rsETH), re-point any entry in LRTConfig's bytes32 contract registry (so it can effectively replace LRTDepositPool, LRTOracle, LRTWithdrawalManager or rsETH itself), and add/remove supported deposit assets and their EigenLayer strategies. Also set as rsETH.custodyAddress, the ASSET_TRANSFER_ROLE-style endpoint stored on the rsETH token. There is no timelock on these operations except for adding new supported assets (which is gated by TIME_LOCK_ROLE / 10-day timelock). In short: this multisig can stop, replace, or reroute every component that mints, burns, or backs rsETH.

receivedPermissions.description: cancel any scheduled but not-yet-executed operation via cancel(id).
queue any call against any target via schedule / scheduleBatch; queued operations become executable after the operation's delay (>= getMinDelay seconds).
grant or revoke any role on this contract.

set eigenLayerRewardReceiver, set maxNegligibleAmount, reassign an asset's EigenLayer strategy (subject to the no-shares-in-prev-strategy check), and remove a supported asset (subject to the dust check).
swap any address in the bytes32 contract or token registries, or the rsETH pointer.

entries.name: KelpPauserMultisig
entries.description: KelpPauserMultisig — secondary pause-only authority. Holds PAUSER_ROLE on LRTConfig. Can pause LRTDepositPool (block new deposits / rsETH minting), LRTOracle (freeze the rsETH/ETH rate and stop fee-mints), LRTWithdrawalManager (block redemptions / rsETH burning), and rsETH transfers. Cannot unpause — that requires MANAGER or DEFAULT_ADMIN. Pure circuit breaker for rsETH user flows.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: fan pause() out to the addresses under LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL, LRT_WITHDRAW_MANAGER, LRT_ORACLE, the rsETH address, and every NodeDelegator returned by getNodeDelegatorQueue(), via pauseAll.

entries.name: PauserRegistry
entries.description: Defines and stores pauser and unpauser roles for EigenLayer contracts.

entries.name: KelpLegacyProtocolTreasury
entries.description: KelpLegacyProtocolTreasury — referenced from FeeReceiver._legacyProtocolTreasury. Historical destination for protocol fees before the current FeeReceiver / PROTOCOL_TREASURY split was introduced. No active write authority over rsETH; kept on-chain for backward reference.

entries.name: TimelockController
entries.description: A timelock that allows scheduling calls and executing or cancelling them with a delay.

entries.name: DurationVaultStrategy
entries.description: EigenLayer duration-vault strategy variant used for time-locked staking positions.

values.explanation: Duration-bound vault strategy with configurable deposit caps and lock periods

entries.name: LRTUnstakingVault
entries.description: Forwarder vault sitting between NodeDelegators (post-EigenLayer-unstake) and the withdrawal manager. Holds whatever LST and ETH balances arrive from NodeDelegators (after their completeUnstaking forwards balance deltas) and from LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL (via ASSET_TRANSFER_ROLE transfers); pushes them out only through redeem(), which is callable solely by the LRT_WITHDRAW_MANAGER-registered address. Inherits PausableUpgradeable but exposes no pause() / unpause() and no function uses whenNotPaused, so the inherited paused field is permanently false. Function gates: onlyLRTWithdrawalManager (msg.sender == lrtConfig.withdrawManager()): redeem (sends asset/ETH to msg.sender; the only outflow path on this contract). onlyLRTNodeDelegator (msg.sender registered in ILRTDepositPool.isNodeDelegator): increaseUncompletedWithdrawalCount, decreaseUncompletedWithdrawalCount. ASSET_TRANSFER_ROLE: transferAssetToNodeDelegator, transferETHToNodeDelegator (ndcIndex resolves through LRTDepositPool.getNodeDelegatorQueue). MANAGER: setMaxUncompletedWithdrawalCount (hard-capped at 80 by the setter), setUncompletedWithdrawalCount (recomputes the counter by iterating LRTDepositPool.getNodeDelegatorQueue and summing EIGEN_DELEGATION_MANAGER.getQueuedWithdrawals lengths). OPERATOR_ROLE: setQueuedWithdrawalsBuffer (per-asset reservation subtracted from balanceOf inside getAssetsAvailableForInstantWithdrawal). Permissionless: receive() (logs only), receiveFromLRTDepositPool and receiveFromNodeDelegator (no-op payable hooks).

lrtConfig.description: Address of the LRTConfig used for every role check (onlyLRTManager, onlyLRTOperator, onlyAssetTransferRole) and every registry lookup (getContract for LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL, LRT_WITHDRAW_MANAGER, EIGEN_DELEGATION_MANAGER plus depositPool() / withdrawManager() / strategyManager() helpers). Set in initialize and not updateable by any function on this contract.

paused.description: Inherited PausableUpgradeable storage. No function on this contract calls _pause / _unpause, no function uses whenNotPaused / whenPaused, and there is no pause() or unpause() entrypoint, so this slot is permanently false on this bytecode.

uncompletedWithdrawalCount.description: Counter of EigenLayer-side withdrawals queued by NodeDelegators that have not yet been completed back into this vault. Incremented by any address registered in LRTDepositPool.isNodeDelegator via increaseUncompletedWithdrawalCount, decremented (clamped at 0) by the same callers via decreaseUncompletedWithdrawalCount, or rewritten to the live total by MANAGER via setUncompletedWithdrawalCount (which sums IDelegationManager.getQueuedWithdrawals lengths across every entry in LRTDepositPool.getNodeDelegatorQueue).

maxUncompletedWithdrawalCount.description: Cap exposed for external callers; not enforced on this contract. NodeDelegator.initiateUnstaking and NodeDelegator.undelegate both read uncompletedWithdrawalCount() and maxUncompletedWithdrawalCount() on this vault and revert if a new queued withdrawal would exceed the cap. Settable only by MANAGER via setMaxUncompletedWithdrawalCount, which itself reverts if the new value exceeds 80.

queuedWithdrawalsBuffer.description: Per-asset value of the queuedWithdrawalsBuffer[asset] mapping: amount of asset on this vault reserved for queued (non-instant) withdrawals and therefore subtracted from balanceOf(asset) inside getAssetsAvailableForInstantWithdrawal. Writeable only by OPERATOR_ROLE via setQueuedWithdrawalsBuffer.

entries.name: KelpManagerMultisig
entries.description: KelpManagerMultisig, operational admin of the rsETH protocol. Holds three roles on LRTConfig. MANAGER lets it set protocolFeeInBPS (the rsETH protocol fee LRTOracle mints to the treasury on every rebase, capped at 1500 bps by the setter) and per-asset deposit limits, tune LRTOracle parameters (maxFeeMintAmountPerDay, the manager-only updateRSETHPriceAsManager that bypasses the price-percentage limit) and LRTWithdrawalManager parameters (withdrawal delay, instant-withdrawal enable/fee/fee-recipient), set rsETH's daily mint cap, blocklist any rsETH holder for 24h via blockUserTransfers, mark addresses as permanently exempt from such freezes, call delegateTo / undelegate / createEigenPod on the NodeDelegators (changes which EigenLayer operator each NodeDelegator delegates to), and call stakeEthForStETH on LRTDepositPool. PAUSER_ROLE lets it pause every Kelp contract via LRTConfig.pauseAll or each individual pause function. OPERATOR_ROLE lets it drive NodeDelegator workflows (depositAssetIntoStrategy, stake32Eth, verifyWithdrawalCredentials, startCheckpoint, initiateUnstaking, processClaim) and the operator-gated paths on LRTDepositPool (internal swaps), LRTWithdrawalManager (completeWithdrawalForUser, depositIdleETHToAave, collectInterestToTreasury, unlockQueue) and LRTUnstakingVault. Cannot grant roles, replace contracts in the LRTConfig registry, set the EigenLayer reward receiver, change the dust threshold, or unpause anything (those are DEFAULT_ADMIN), and cannot move pooled LST/ETH balances between LRTDepositPool, NodeDelegators and LRTUnstakingVault (that is ASSET_TRANSFER_ROLE).

receivedPermissions.description: fan pause() out to the addresses under LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL, LRT_WITHDRAW_MANAGER, LRT_ORACLE, the rsETH address, and every NodeDelegator returned by getNodeDelegatorQueue(), via pauseAll.
set protocolFeeInBPS (capped at 15%) and update depositLimitByAsset for any supported asset.

entries.name: WrappedTokenGatewayV3

entries.name: FeeReceiver
entries.description: Treasury that receives protocol fees. Fees are minted as rsETH to this address by LRTOracle.updateRSETHPrice() — the rebase function takes protocolFeeInBPS of the per-period reward and mints the equivalent rsETH here (subject to a daily fee-mint cap).

entries.name: NodeDelegator_A5
entries.description: Per-instance EigenLayer staker: holds an EigenPod for native-ETH restaking, ERC20 strategy shares for LST restaking, and delegates the resulting stake to a single EigenLayer operator via DelegationManager. Role checks consult IAccessControl on lrtConfig. Role gates: OPERATOR_ROLE: depositAssetIntoStrategy (deposits this contract's full balance of asset into IStrategy(lrtConfig.assetStrategy(asset)) via EIGEN_STRATEGY_MANAGER, recipient is address(this)), stake32Eth and stake32EthValidated (each consume 32 ether of this contract's ETH balance to register a validator via EigenPodManager.stake; withdrawal credentials derived from eigenPod, pubkey deduplicated through PUBKEY_REGISTRY, stakedButUnverifiedNativeETH += 32 ether), verifyWithdrawalCredentials (proves validators against eigenPod, stakedButUnverifiedNativeETH -= 32 ether per validator), startCheckpoint, processClaim (recipient is lrtConfig.eigenLayerRewardReceiver()), initiateUnstaking (withdrawer is address(this), bumps LRT_UNSTAKING_VAULT.uncompletedWithdrawalCount), completeUnstaking (forwards the resulting balance delta to LRT_UNSTAKING_VAULT when receiveAsTokens). MANAGER: delegateTo (caller picks the EigenLayer operator and supplies the operator's approverSignatureAndExpiry), createEigenPod (calls EigenPodManager.createPod and writes eigenPod, reverts if already set), undelegate (auto-queues one forced withdrawal per active strategy), maxApproveToEigenStrategyManager / revokeApprovalToEigenStrategyManager. ASSET_TRANSFER_ROLE: transferBackToLRTDepositPool, transferETHToLRTUnstakingVault. PAUSER_ROLE: pause. DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE: unpause. Caller-restricted: sendETHFromDepositPoolToNDC requires msg.sender == LRT_DEPOSIT_POOL, sendETHFromUnstakingVaultToNDC requires msg.sender == LRT_UNSTAKING_VAULT, receive() accepts ETH from any caller. eigenPod is also written lazily by stake32Eth on the first call when it is still zero (read from EigenPodManager.ownerToPod). whenNotPaused gates only depositAssetIntoStrategy, stake32Eth, processClaim, undelegate, initiateUnstaking, completeUnstaking, transferBackToLRTDepositPool and transferETHToLRTUnstakingVault; delegateTo, createEigenPod, verifyWithdrawalCredentials, startCheckpoint, the approve helpers, pause and unpause execute regardless of paused state.

Set in initialize and not updateable by any function on this contract.

Blocks only the whenNotPaused-gated subset of functions (depositAssetIntoStrategy, stake32Eth, processClaim, undelegate, initiateUnstaking, completeUnstaking, transferBackToLRTDepositPool, transferETHToLRTUnstakingVault); MANAGER's delegateTo / createEigenPod / approve helpers and OPERATOR's verifyWithdrawalCredentials / startCheckpoint continue to execute while paused.

eigenPod.description: EigenPod owned by this NodeDelegator and used for native-ETH restaking. Written by createEigenPod (MANAGER, reverts if already set) or lazily by stake32Eth (OPERATOR, when still zero, by reading EigenPodManager.ownerToPod(address(this))). Once set, the field is not cleared by any function on this contract. Used as the verify / checkpoint target by verifyWithdrawalCredentials and startCheckpoint, and as the implicit destination of validator beacon-chain withdrawals (the validator's withdrawal credentials are determined by the EigenPod, not by callers of stake32Eth).

elOperatorDelegatedTo.description: EigenLayer operator this NodeDelegator's restaked stake is currently delegated to. Not a stored value; resolved live by calling EIGEN_DELEGATION_MANAGER.delegatedTo(address(this)). Changed only by MANAGER, via delegateTo (sets it to a caller-supplied operator that has approved this delegation, either by having no approver set or by signing the supplied approverSignatureAndExpiry) or undelegate (clears it and queues one forced withdrawal per active strategy).

stakedButUnverifiedNativeETH.description: Running total (in wei) of ETH staked into beacon-chain validators via stake32Eth whose withdrawal credentials have not yet been proven against the EigenPod. Incremented by exactly 32 ether on each successful stake32Eth and decremented by 32 ether per validator on each successful verifyWithdrawalCredentials. Read by getEffectivePodShares as part of the ETH backing this NDC reports to the rest of the protocol; an unproven-or-stuck validator therefore keeps inflating this counter and the reported pod shares until verifyWithdrawalCredentials lands.

getEffectivePodShares.description: View returning stakedButUnverifiedNativeETH plus the withdrawable share read from IStrategy(lrtConfig.beaconChainETHStrategy()) for this contract. Represents the ETH-equivalent native-restaking position this NDC contributes to the protocol-wide TVL. The withdrawable-share component is read from EigenLayer state and reflects beacon-chain balance changes (rewards, slashing, exits) at call time.

entries.name: KelpRewardReceiverMultisig
entries.description: KelpRewardReceiverMultisig — set as LRTConfig.eigenLayerRewardReceiver. Receives EigenLayer rewards earned by Kelp's NodeDelegators. Does not directly mint or burn rsETH, but holds the reward value that ultimately backs rsETH; how this multisig redistributes those rewards (e.g. forwarding into LRTDepositPool) is what makes the rewards actually flow into the rsETH backing.
Native ETH sentinel address used by LRTConfig.supportedAssetList to mark native ETH as an accepted deposit (not a real contract).

entries.name: EigenPod
entries.description: EigenLayer EigenPod, one per staker. podOwner is set in initialize when the BeaconProxy is deployed by EigenPodManager and is not changeable afterwards. Validators staked through this pod have withdrawal credentials hardcoded to address(this), so beacon-chain withdrawals always exit into this pod. Function gates: onlyEigenPodManager: stake (msg.value must equal 32 ether, calls ETHPOS.deposit with this pod's withdrawal credentials), withdrawRestakedBeaconChainETH (sends ETH to a manager-supplied recipient, capped at withdrawableRestakedExecutionLayerGwei). onlyEigenPodOwner: recoverTokens (sweeps arbitrary ERC20 tokens to a caller-supplied recipient; does not touch native ETH or restaked balance accounting), setProofSubmitter. onlyOwnerOrProofSubmitter: startCheckpoint, verifyWithdrawalCredentials, requestConsolidation (forwards a fee + pubkey-pair payload to the EIP-7251 consolidation predeploy), requestWithdrawal (forwards a fee + pubkey/amount payload to the EIP-7002 withdrawal predeploy). Permissionless: receive() (accepts ETH from any caller, logs only), verifyCheckpointProofs (any caller advances the active checkpoint with valid Merkle proofs), verifyStaleBalance (any caller can mark a validator with stale balance to require a re-checkpoint). Each non-Manager entrypoint is independently gated by IPausable(eigenPodManager).paused(index) with a function-specific pause index, so EigenLayer's pause governance on EigenPodManager controls each operation separately.

podOwner.description: The pod's owner. Set once by initialize when EigenPodManager deploys this BeaconProxy and not updateable by any function on this contract. Powers on this contract: recoverTokens (sweep arbitrary ERC20 tokens lying on this contract to a caller-supplied recipient), setProofSubmitter (set the proofSubmitter address), startCheckpoint, verifyWithdrawalCredentials, requestConsolidation (EIP-7251 consolidation predeploy), requestWithdrawal (EIP-7002 withdrawal predeploy, can request a partial or full beacon-chain validator exit but the resulting ETH lands in this pod). Cannot withdraw the pod's restaked ETH directly: withdrawRestakedBeaconChainETH is onlyEigenPodManager and chooses the recipient itself.

proofSubmitter.description: Optional second address allowed to call the onlyOwnerOrProofSubmitter subset: startCheckpoint, verifyWithdrawalCredentials, requestConsolidation, requestWithdrawal. Cannot recoverTokens, cannot setProofSubmitter. Settable only by podOwner via setProofSubmitter; defaults to address(0), which makes those four entrypoints podOwner-only.

eigenPodManager.description: Immutable, set in the constructor. The single EigenLayer EigenPodManager: sole caller permitted to invoke stake() and withdrawRestakedBeaconChainETH(), and the source of pause status read by every IPausable(eigenPodManager).paused(index) check.

ethPOS.description: Immutable, set in the constructor. Beacon-chain deposit contract that stake() forwards 32 ether to with this pod's withdrawal credentials.

activeValidatorCount.description: Number of validators currently in VALIDATOR_STATUS.ACTIVE. Incremented in verifyWithdrawalCredentials when a new validator's credentials are proven against this pod; decremented (validator transitions to WITHDRAWN) inside verifyCheckpointProofs when a proof shows balance == 0.

currentCheckpoint.description: In-progress checkpoint state. proofsRemaining counts ACTIVE validators not yet checkpointed; podBalanceGwei pins the pod's execution-layer ETH balance at checkpoint start; balanceDeltasGwei accumulates per-validator balance changes proven during the checkpoint; prevBeaconBalanceGwei accumulates each ACTIVE validator's last-known balance. Active iff currentCheckpointTimestamp != 0; cleared when proofsRemaining reaches 0 and the checkpoint finalizes.

currentCheckpointTimestamp.description: Beacon timestamp the active checkpoint was pinned against, or 0 if no checkpoint is active. Set by startCheckpoint, cleared on finalization. While non-zero, verifyWithdrawalCredentials calls require beaconTimestamp > currentCheckpointTimestamp.

lastCheckpointTimestamp.description: Beacon timestamp of the most recently finalized checkpoint. Used as the lower bound for verifyWithdrawalCredentials (beaconTimestamp must exceed it) and as the default lastCheckpointedAt for newly-proven validators when no checkpoint is currently active.

withdrawableRestakedExecutionLayerGwei.description: View returning the internal restakedExecutionLayerGwei: ETH (in gwei) sitting in this pod that has been moved from the beacon chain to the execution layer via finalized checkpoints / exits. Available for withdrawRestakedBeaconChainETH (callable only by eigenPodManager) up to this amount.

getConsolidationRequestFee.description: Live read of the EIP-7251 consolidation predeploy's current fee. Updated per-block by the consensus layer; quoted by requestConsolidation.

getWithdrawalRequestFee.description: Live read of the EIP-7002 withdrawal predeploy's current fee. Updated per-block by the consensus layer; quoted by requestWithdrawal.

entries.name: KelpOperatorEOA
entries.description:
KelpOperatorEOA — single externally-owned account holding OPERATOR_ROLE on LRTConfig (one of three holders, alongside KelpManagerMultisig and a second EOA). Does not directly mint or burn rsETH, but as an OPERATOR can drive every NodeDelegator workflow that moves Kelp's pooled LSTs into and out of EigenLayer: deposit assets into EigenLayer strategies, queue and complete EigenLayer withdrawals, claim rewards, and register beacon-chain validators through EigenPods. The full LST/ETH backing of rsETH is exposed to whoever controls this single private key — slashing or operational disruption from this key propagates into the rsETH/ETH exchange rate computed by LRTOracle.

If this EOA was hacked, the adversary could:
- Call NodeDelegator.stake32Eth with attacker-chosen validator pubkey/signature, registering 32-ETH validators whose consensus keys the attacker controls. Withdrawal credentials are hardcoded to the EigenPod, so the principal cannot be stolen directly — but the attacker can force slashing (signing conflicting attestations / proposals → up to ~1 ETH lost per validator), siphon block proposal tips and MEV, or refuse to attest (inactivity-leak drain). Each event reduces the ETH backing rsETH and lowers the LRTOracle-reported rate.
- Call NodeDelegator.initiateUnstaking on any subset of strategies/shares to queue large EigenLayer withdrawals (7–14 day cooldown). The withdrawer is hardcoded to the NodeDelegator so funds cannot be redirected, but an adversary can deliberately starve LRTWithdrawalManager of redemption liquidity, force users onto the costly Aave-backed instant path, and create a sustained discount on rsETH's secondary-market price.
- Call NodeDelegator.depositAssetIntoStrategy to lock pooled LSTs into the configured EigenLayer strategy at adversarial moments (e.g. just before a known slashing event on that strategy).
- Call NodeDelegator.processClaim with malformed or stale Merkle proofs to grief reward distribution. Rewards still flow to lrtConfig.eigenLayerRewardReceiver (set by MANAGER), so the destination cannot be redirected.
- Call NodeDelegator.startCheckpoint / verifyWithdrawalCredentials at adversarial timings to crystallise slashing losses into rsETH's accounting earlier than otherwise.

What the adversary cannot do with only this key: change the EigenLayer operator delegation (gated by MANAGER), redirect unstaked LSTs or rewards (recipients hardcoded to NodeDelegator / LRTConfig-stored), mint or burn rsETH, pause Kelp contracts, grant or revoke roles, or replace any contract in LRTConfig's registry. Net effect: cannot directly steal rsETH backing, but can systematically destroy value through slashing and liquidity disruption until KelpAdminMultisig revokes the role.
